financial.definition: A financial institution that accepts deposits and provides loans.
financial.example: I deposited money in the bank.

river.definition: The sloping land alongside a body of water.
river.example: We sat near the river bank.

animal.definition: A nocturnal flying mammal that uses echolocation.

sports.definition: A club or stick used for hitting a ball in sports like cricket or baseball.
sports.example: He hit the ball with a bat.

contest.definition: A competitive game or contest between two sides.
contest.example: The football match ended in a draw.

fire_starter.definition: A short thin stick tipped with a chemical that ignites when struck.
fire_starter.example: He struck a match to light the candle.

direction.definition: The side of the body to the west when facing north; opposite of right.

departed.definition: Past tense of leave; went away from a place.

direction.definition: The side opposite to left; toward the east when facing north.
direction.example: Take a right turn at the intersection.

document.definition: A collection of documents or data stored together; to organize records.

tool.definition: A hand tool with a rough surface used for smoothing or shaping materials.
tool.example: Use a file to smooth the edge.

jewelry.definition: A small circular band worn on a finger as ornament or symbol.
jewelry.example: She wore a beautiful diamond ring.

sound.definition: To make a clear resonant or vibrating sound.

illumination.definition: Electromagnetic radiation that makes things visible; a source of illumination.

observe.definition: To look at or observe something attentively over time.
observe.example: Let us watch the movie together.

timepiece.definition: A small portable timepiece typically worn on the wrist.
timepiece.example: He checked his watch for the time.

animal.definition: A fish-eating aquatic mammal with flippers that lives in cold waters.
animal.example: The seal was resting on the ice.

closure.definition: A device or substance used to close or secure something tightly.
closure.example: Break the seal to open the package.

lock_opener.definition: A shaped piece of metal used to open or close a lock.

crucial.definition: Of crucial importance; essential to achieving something.
crucial.example: Hard work is the key to success.

recreation.definition: A large public area of land used for recreation with grass, trees, and paths.

vehicle.definition: To bring a vehicle to a halt and leave it temporarily in a place.

present_time.definition: Belonging to the present time; happening or existing now.

flow.definition: A continuous flow of water, air, or electricity in a definite direction.
flow.example: The ocean current was very strong.

season.definition: The season between winter and summer when plants begin to grow.

coil.definition: An elastic device, typically a metal coil, that returns to shape after compression.

water_source.definition: A natural source where water flows out from the ground.
water_source.example: We drank from a mountain spring.

bird.definition: A tall, long-legged, long-necked wading bird.
bird.example: The crane flew gracefully over the lake.

machine.definition: A large, tall machine used for moving heavy objects by lifting and lowering them.
machine.example: The construction crane lifted the steel beams.

bird.definition: A waterbird with a broad flat bill, short legs, and webbed feet.
bird.example: The duck swam across the pond.

action.definition: To lower the head or body quickly to avoid something.
action.example: Duck down to avoid the low beam.

tree.definition: The tough outer covering of the trunk and branches of a tree.
tree.example: The bark of the old oak was rough.

dog_sound.definition: The sharp explosive sound made by a dog.
dog_sound.example: The dog began to bark at strangers.

fastener.definition: A small metal spike with a broadened flat head, hammered into wood to join things.
fastener.example: He hammered a nail into the wall.

body_part.definition: The horny covering on the upper surface of the tip of a finger or toe.

calendar.definition: A specific day of the month or year as expressed by a number.

fruit.definition: A sweet, dark brown oval fruit with a hard stone inside.
fruit.example: She ate a date as a snack.

romantic.definition: A social or romantic appointment or outing with another person.
romantic.example: They went on a date last night.

container.definition: A cylindrical metal container for food or drinks.
container.example: He opened a can of soda.

ability.definition: To be able to; to have the ability or capacity to do something.

insect.definition: A small winged insect, often considered a pest.
insect.example: A fly was buzzing around the kitchen.

travel_air.definition: To move through the air using wings or in an aircraft.
travel_air.example: We will fly to Paris tomorrow.

stone.definition: A solid mineral material forming part of the earth's surface.
stone.example: He sat on a large rock by the river.

music.definition: A genre of popular music with a strong beat, typically played with electric guitars.
music.example: She loves listening to rock music.

motion.definition: To move gently back and forth or from side to side.
motion.example: She rocked the baby to sleep.

water_wave.definition: A long body of water curling and breaking on the shore.
water_wave.example: The waves crashed against the shore.

gesture.definition: To move one's hand to and fro in greeting or as a signal.
gesture.example: She waved goodbye from the window.

vehicle.definition: A series of connected railway carriages pulled by a locomotive.
vehicle.example: The train arrived at the station on time.

practice.definition: To teach or learn a particular skill or behavior through practice.
practice.example: He trains every day for the marathon.

hit.definition: To hit forcibly and deliberately with one's hand or a weapon.
hit.example: He struck the ball with great force.

protest.definition: An organized refusal to work as a form of protest.
protest.example: The workers went on strike for better wages.

mail.definition: A written or printed message sent by mail or delivered to someone.
mail.example: I received a letter from my friend.

alphabet.definition: A character representing a sound in an alphabet.
alphabet.example: The letter A is the first in the alphabet.

just.definition: Treating people equally and according to rules; just and reasonable.
just.example: The decision was fair to everyone.

event.definition: A gathering of stalls and amusements for public entertainment.
event.example: We went to the county fair last weekend.

location.definition: A particular spot, place, or position in an area or on a surface.
location.example: We agreed to meet at the same point.

tip.definition: The tapered, sharp end of a tool, weapon, or other object.
tip.example: The point of the needle was very sharp.

argument.definition: An idea, opinion, or piece of information put forward in a discussion.
argument.example: She made an excellent point during the debate.

line.definition: A number of people or things arranged in a straight line.
line.example: They sat in the front row.

argument.example: The neighbors had a terrible row last night.

boat.definition: To propel a boat through water using oars.
boat.example: Let us row across the lake.

reading.definition: A written or printed work consisting of bound pages.
reading.example: I am reading a fascinating book.

reserve.definition: To arrange in advance to have or use something; to make a reservation.
reserve.example: Please book a table for two.

sound_level.definition: The degree of loudness of a sound.

book_series.definition: A single book forming part of a larger work or series.
book_series.example: Volume two of the series is the best.

space.definition: The amount of space that a substance or object occupies.
space.example: Calculate the volume of the cylinder.

alter.definition: To make or become different; to modify or transform.
alter.example: We need to change the plan.

coins.definition: Coins as opposed to paper currency; money returned after payment.
coins.example: Do you have change for a dollar?

written.definition: A brief written message or record of something.
written.example: She left a note on the desk.

music.definition: A single tone of a definite pitch made by a musical instrument or voice.
music.example: He played the wrong note on the piano.

tree.definition: The main woody stem of a tree.
tree.example: The trunk of the tree was massive.

car.definition: The enclosed space at the back of a car for carrying luggage.
car.example: Put the bags in the trunk.

elephant.definition: The elongated nose of an elephant used for breathing, grasping, and feeding.
elephant.example: The elephant lifted food with its trunk.

clothing.definition: A set of matching outer clothes consisting of a jacket and trousers or skirt.
clothing.example: He wore a suit to the interview.

legal.definition: A lawsuit; a legal action brought before a court.
legal.example: She filed a suit against the company.

throw.definition: To throw or toss something, especially a ball.
throw.example: The pitcher threw a perfect pitch.

sound_quality.definition: The quality of a sound governed by the rate of vibrations; highness or lowness.
sound_quality.example: Her voice had a very high pitch.

sales.definition: A presentation or proposal designed to persuade someone to buy or invest.
sales.example: He gave a great sales pitch.

story.definition: The main events of a novel, play, movie, or similar work.
story.example: The plot of the movie was thrilling.

land.definition: A small piece of ground marked out for a specific purpose.
land.example: They bought a plot of land to build a house.

scheme.definition: A secret plan to do something harmful or illegal.
scheme.example: They plotted to overthrow the government.

guide.definition: To show the way; to be in charge or command of.
guide.example: She will lead the team to victory.

metal.definition: A heavy, soft, bluish-gray metallic element.
metal.example: Old pipes were made of lead.

body_part.definition: A part of the body that has a specific vital function.
body_part.example: The heart is a vital organ.

instrument.definition: A large musical keyboard instrument with pipes supplied by bellows.
instrument.example: She played the organ at church.

music_group.definition: A group of musicians who play music together.
music_group.example: The band played at the concert.

strip.definition: A thin flat strip or loop of material used for fastening or as decoration.
strip.example: She tied her hair with a band.

weight.definition: A unit of weight equal to approximately 0.4536 kilograms.

currency.definition: The basic monetary unit of the United Kingdom.
